body: PT Dua Putra Utama Makmur Tbk (DPUM) plans to expand its export market to China, Canada, and the United States by 2025, aiming to increase revenue, as fish exports to Japan currently account for over 90% of its revenue. In Q3 2024, DPUM reported a 28% year-on-year revenue increase to IDR 775.82 billion, with fish exports contributing IDR 663.71 billion, or 85.55% of total revenue.
title: PT Dua Putra Utama Makmur to Expand Export Market to China, Canada, and US by 2025.

body: PT J Resources Asia Pasifik TBK (PSAB) expects to reach revenue of USD 230-240 million in 2024, a 40% increase from last year, driven by higher gold demand and production, which is projected to reach 100 thousand ounces by the end of 2024. The company's sales have already increased 86.78% year-on-year to USD 173.86 million as of September 2024, and its new Doup gold mine project is expected to generate USD 3 billion in revenue over its 14-year lifetime.
title: PT J Resources Asia Pasifik TBK Expects Significant Revenue and Gold Production Growth in 2024

body: Pemberton Asian Opportunities Fund increased its share ownership in PT Sarimelati Kencana Tbk (PZZA) to 6.29% from 4.96% by purchasing 40 million shares at IDR 128 per share, valued at IDR 5.12 billion, bringing its total share ownership to 190 million shares. Following the transaction, PZZA's shares corrected 6.29% to IDR 119 on Tuesday afternoon, from IDR 127 per share when the transaction took place.
title: Pemberton Asian Opportunities Fund increases stake in PT Sarimelati Kencana, manager of Pizza Hut outlets, to 6.29%.

body: PT Bumi Serpong Damai Tbk (BSDE) has made a tender offer to acquire 382.01 million shares of PT Suryamas Dutamakmur Tbk (SMDM) at a price of IDR 531 per share, valuing the transaction at IDR 202.85 billion. This acquisition will increase BSDE's control over SMDM to 91.99%, adding 29.86 hectares of ready-to-sell land reserves to its portfolio, following a previous purchase of 4.39 billion SMDM shares at IDR 318 per share from Top Global Limited.
title: PT Bumi Serpong Damai to Acquire 8.01% Stake in PT Suryamas Dutamakmur through Tender Offer Worth IDR 202.85 Billion.

body: Indonesia's trade balance surplus decreased by 14.12% to $28.85 billion during January-November 2024, compared to $33.60 billion in the same period in 2023, driven by a 3.45% increase in oil and gas trade balance deficit and a 7.98% decrease in non-oil and gas trade balance surplus. Total exports during the 11-month period reached $241.25 billion, while total imports amounted to $212.39 billion, resulting in a trade balance surplus of $28.85 billion.
title: Indonesia's trade balance surplus decreases 14.12% to $28.85 billion in January-November 2024.

body: Schroders (SDR) is reportedly closing its 30-year-old Indonesian business as part of a restructuring effort to cut non-performing lines, with the company aiming to divest its Indonesian assets, worth approximately $4 billion, and is currently in talks with potential buyers, including HSBC (HSBC), Allianz, and PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BNI). The company has assigned UBS (UBS) as an advisor for the divestment, and while valuation is yet to be determined, Schroders aims to continue offering exceptional services to its clients.
title: Schroders to close its 30-year-old Indonesian business as part of global restructuring.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index fell 0.9% or 66.16 index points to 7,258.63, with a transaction value of IDR 11.78 trillion (USD 735 million), significantly lower than the weekly average of IDR 20.19 trillion (USD 1.27 billion). Foreign investors recorded a net sell of IDR 621.66 billion, with PT Telkom Indonesia Tbk (TLKM) being the top laggard, while PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) drove the index up, contributing to a 10.36 index point gain.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Falls 0.9% Amid Low Liquidity and Foreign Sell-Off.

body: PT Lautan Luas Tbk (LTLS) reported a 120% year-on-year increase in net profit attributable to the parent company, reaching IDR 183 billion in September 2024, driven by a 6.38% year-over-year rise in revenue to IDR 5.73 trillion. The company's growth is attributed to its long-term strategy of diversifying and expanding its product portfolio, which has led to increased gross profit margins of 17.82% in September 2024.
title: PT Lautan Luas reports 120% year-on-year net profit increase to IDR 183 billion in Q3 2024.

body: Foreign investors bought a net 1.29 billion shares from 946 issuers, with digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) ranking first on the top buy list with a net volume of 1.44 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign investors sold shares of state-owned banks PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) and PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BBNI), as well as PT Telkom Indonesia Tbk (TLKM), resulting in net volumes of -81.22 million, -25.72 million, and -60.46 million shares, respectively.
title: Foreign investors buy 1.29 billion more shares than they sell in Indonesian market, led by digital company GoTo and property firm Lippo Karawaci.

body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) recorded a net foreign share purchase of IDR 1.44 billion, the highest among all issuers in the capital market, with foreign buys of IDR 3.23 billion and foreign sells of IDR 1.79 billion. The company's share price fluctuated throughout the day, closing down IDR 4 at IDR 72 per share, with a total trading volume of 7.28 billion shares worth IDR 534.60 billion.
title: Goto Gojek Tokopedia tops net foreign shares with 1.44 billion, surpassing all other issuers in the capital market.

body: The Indonesian government has announced a 12% value-added tax (VAT) increase, effective January 1, 2025, which will only apply to luxury or premium goods and services, such as high-quality rice, meat, and premium education and health services. The government will exempt basic commodities like rice, meat, fish, eggs, and vegetables from VAT, and will also provide incentives to sectors like property, hybrid cars, and MSMES, as well as food and electricity assistance.
title: Indonesian government announces 12% value-added tax increase on luxury goods and services starting January 1, 2025.

body: Jon Fieris reduced his shares in PT Hotel Fitra International Tbk (HFIT) by 9.2% over two weeks in December 2024, with a total sale transaction value of IDR 11.47 billion, resulting in his ownership falling from 18.55% to 9.35%. The transactions involved the sale of 120 million shares at IDR 95 and IDR 96 per share, respectively, with the total value of the transactions recorded at IDR 4.75 billion and IDR 6.72 billion.
title: Jon Fieris reduces stake in PT Hotel Fitra International Tbk by 9.2% through IDR 11.47 billion share sale.

body: PT Hotel Candi Baru purchased an additional 5.25 billion shares of PT Industri Jamu dan Farmasi Sido Muncul Tbk (SIDO), increasing its stake to 59.5%, while UOB Kay Hian Private Limited acquired 5.08 billion shares of PT Soho Global Health Tbk, becoming a controlling shareholder with a 40% stake. Meanwhile, Garibaldi Thohir invested in 450.36 million shares of PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia Tbk (AADI), increasing PT Adaro Strategic Investments' ownership to 41.1%, and foreign investors made notable transactions in other companies, including PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk, PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI), and PT Meratus Jasa Prima Tbk.
title: Multiple investors make significant share purchases and sales in various Indonesian companies, including herbal medicine manufacturers and energy firms.

body: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) has announced an interim dividend of IDR 20.46 trillion, or IDR 135 per share, a 60.7% year-over-year increase, which will be distributed on January 15, 2025. The bank's strong financial performance, including a 2.43% year-over-year increase in net profit and a 4.57% increase in unrestricted retained earnings, has boosted its stock price by 1.19% to IDR 4,220 per share.
title: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia's Interim Dividend Increases 60.7% Year-on-Year to IDR 20.46 Trillion.

body: PT Palma Serasih Tbk (PSGO) has acquired 100% of PT Sabhantara Rawi Sentosa from PT Dhanistha Surya Nusantara and PT Surya Nusantara Sawitindo, with a transaction value not exceeding IDR 467.4 billion, or 20% of PSGO's total equity of IDR 2.33 trillion as of September 2024. The acquisition, carried out through PSGO's subsidiary PT Anugerah Energitama, adds 620,718 shares to PSGO's portfolio, with the company's stock price rising from IDR 192 to IDR 194 since the announcement.
title: PT Palma Serasih acquires palm plantation in East Kalimantan through purchase of PT Sabhantara Rawi Sentosa.

body: PT Sumber Alfaria Trijaya Tbk (AMRT) has closed around 400 unprofitable outlets since the beginning of the year, citing high lease costs and low sales, but plans to open 800 new outlets by the end of the year, with the number of new openings exceeding closures. The company reported revenue of IDR 88.22 trillion and net profit of IDR 2.39 trillion in the first nine months of 2024, and aims to maintain its financial performance despite the outlet closures.
title: Alfamart closes 400 unprofitable outlets but plans to open 800 new ones by year-end.

body: PT Trimegah Bangun Persada Tbk (NCKL) acquired a 10% stake in PT Obi Nickel Cobalt from Li Yuen for IDR 2.1 trillion (USD 140 million), increasing its ownership to 20% from 10%. The acquisition is categorized as an affiliate transaction, with Li Yuen being the ultimate beneficial owner of NCKL's shares, and comes amid a week of mass divestment of NCKL's shares by foreign investors, totaling IDR 2.76 billion.
title: PT Trimegah Bangun Persada acquires 10% of PT Obi Nickel Cobalt shares from Li Yuen for USD 140 million.
